title: Velvety Vietnamese Tofu Soup
description: A silky, umami-packed tofu soup with rapeseed oil that warms the soul and energizes the day.

ingredients.name: silken tofu
ingredients.notes: cut into 1.5 cm cubes, keep chilled until poaching

instructions: Heat rapeseed oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat until shimmering.
Add sliced onion, minced garlic, and ginger; sauté for 3 minutes until fragrant and onion translucent.
Stir in julienned carrot and sliced mushrooms; cook for 2 minutes, letting the vegetables soften.
Pour in vegetable broth and bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes to infuse flavors.
Add soy sauce, rice wine vinegar, sesame oil, and sugar; taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Fold in silken tofu cubes, being careful not to break them, and simmer for another 3 minutes until tofu is heated through.
In a small bowl, whisk cornstarch with water to create a slurry.
Gradually pour the slurry into the soup while stirring; cook for 1 minute until the soup thickens slightly and becomes velvety.
Turn off the heat and ladle the soup into bowls.
Garnish each bowl with chopped green onion and fresh cilantro before serving.
